reviews.review_text:
Look, I’ve seen this game, Rain World. Many people are talking about it. Tremendous game, really. Strong, tough people come up to me, big tears in their eyes, and they say, "Sir, the rain is terrible." And it’s true! It’s the worst rain in the history of our country. Maybe ever. Very unfair rain. A total disaster.
And let me tell you about this ecosystem. They’re letting anybody in! Lizards of all colors. Green, pink, the nasty red ones... bad hombres. And you know what they’re doing? They’re eating the cats! They’re eating the slugcats! They’re eating the pets of the people that live there! It's a disgrace.
Nobody is doing anything. This guy, "Five Pebbles"... a total loser. Very low IQ Iterator. He has absolutely no control over his borders, vultures are just dropping out of the sky illegally. The karma system is totally rigged against us, believe me.
They give you a little pointy stick. I said, "A stick? Where is the tremendous artillery?" Sad!
It’s a tough game. But I beat it. Very easily, by the way. My slugcat was a stable genius. Huge success. But for regular people? Very sad.
Rating: 10/10 for me, 0/10 for the radical left lizards. MAKE THE SLUGCAT GREAT AGAIN!
Honestly I haven't been as obsessed with a fantasy world since Bionicle which was my childhood.

Looking back, marketing for this game is abysmal. I get that you want to get in touch with your community, but as far as I am concerned, this game deserved far better marketing than what we got so it could have touched people outside of its demographic circle and I'm gonna try to do that for my friends that still have not played it.

Rain World is a 2D survival platformer where you're playing as the lowest of the food chain trying to survive a wildlife that has outlasted its cycle of life while making sense of a long lost world in ruin and your life in it.
It will not be easy.
You won't fully understand what you're doing at first.
You will get lost and die a lot by the most spine-chilling and cortisol-inducing creatures you have ever seen.
You WILL find it unfair.

And that's because it is, your odds at succeeding are really small. It's not a conventional game by any means, a lot of its story themes and game mechanics might seem cryptic at first, the control scheme is weird (but you get used to it after some time) and ennemies are moving in a kind of erratic and unpredictable way. I really like all of this to be honest, might be one of the only games out there that literally says "♥♥♥♥ it we ball" by design.

And yet, all of that is contrasted by the most beautifully crafted and heartbreakingly rich fictionnal universe in sync with the fantastic gems and colossal amounts of soundtracks, absolutely massive interconnected map, insane procedural animations, deep movement mechanics and item interaction systems, and pixel art visuals a game could ever have.

Rain World is simply put, my favorite game of all time.

At its core, it is disguised as a live action roleplaying nature documentary with lots of advanced tech for experienced players... But you will get better and get through it and when you start to peel its layers, you will realise that deep down, it is in fact about surviving loss, enduring hardships and friendships, what you can (or cannot) make of it and the beauty of it all.

I have basically done everything and anything this game, its DLCs (and its modding community) could ever offer and I still come back to it from time to time to immerse myself in its mysterious and beautiful world.

One thing I also get from other people is that: it is not a game for everyone, the journey from start to finish is exhiliratingly painful and even if you're the most tolerant gamer to ever exist, this game will make you ragequit more than once. But if you decide to stick with it just a bit longer, as you play, you might start to uncover its well hidden secrets waiting behind this thin veil for you to open. And once this happens, you will realise that it is an absolute one-of-a-kind gem of a masterpiece that everyone should at least experience once.

(SPOILERS FOR ALL VANILLA ENDINGS)

There are many things I can say about this game.
Been playin it before downpour released... on console, of course.
For a few years, I've had this game...

...and yet the ending never gets old.

The long trek you must make to get to the end.
Every second spent walking, there is nothing else you think of besides this moment.
Wandering around in darkness
Sometimes illuminated by a wave of light
To call it 'gold' is but a mockery.
Once you make that fall
Once you plummet
You understand that there is no going back
Deep into the bowels of the land you go
Crawling through the crevices, no longer surrounded by intricate architecture
The glow swirls along with the dark
Deep into the rock
Until you find it
An empty pool of void,
yet so full.
Once you descend
All you can do
Is go down

Down

down

down




...and then

it's up

up

up

up you go

what was once a dim glow

becomes something akin to the blinding light of heaven

you let it envelop you...





...and after the cutscene...





you're back at the campaign selections


seeing that silhouette of a slugcat



...some may say that this is the end of the game....






...but what if once you have ascended,

you have truly came to another plane of existence





you have woken up






again a part of the real world.
No other game can make me feel like an 8-year-old boy in a rural village in central Poland alone with his local pastor like this game.

You might have read that Rain World is not for everyone, and they are right! Rain World was not for me. I tried a few times to enjoy the game, but unfortunately, it didn't click with me. I admit, the concept is original, but the gameplay felt too clunky. Add to that the high difficulty and lack of guidance, and the experience ended up being a little too frustrating for my taste. I'd say that if you're curious, give it a shot, cause it has great reviews and it's a unique game. I hope you'll like it better than I did!

I'm only 70 cycles in, so I still have a long way to go. This is the type of game that, even though I really love it, I would only recommend it to my worst enemy. It is really, REALLY difficult and rather stress-inducing, so much that it forces me to go outside just to distract myself from the horrors that lie within this game. The game is really pretty though, and the ecosystem is fascinating to watch and be apart of.

This review is marked negative but I want to specify that it is more or less just to specify that it is not the type of game for me.

I absolutely like the concept, the survival, the strategies used to avoid predators. The artstyle. The soundtrack specifying the type of predators. The predator interactions.

But the gameplay essentially boils down to two things

1. Eating enough to hibernate
2. Platforming

That's it. I played 8 hours to see if there was more, but aside from environmental consumables to get around or fend of predators, there's nothing else. The "end goal" of Rainworld is just continuing that cycle. 

If you like that type of game, you will like it.
Truly special game, one of a kind. would recommend to all masochist and non masochist that like to explore.

O sea está bien, el RNG en general es muy frustrante, siempre va a haber altas chances de morir injustamente. Lo que en realidad quiere el juego es que te familiarices con los entornos, mecanicas y anticipes lo peor en todo momento, subiendo así la probabilidad de sobrevivir ante situaciones extremas. Jugar con un mapa le quita bastante de su esencia (ya que es por sobre todo un juego de exploración) pero jugar sin mapa es como pegarse un disparo en la pinchila.

new favorite game

i still suck at it but i find it really fun to have my fight or flight insticts take over me when i play.
idk how to progress forward through the game but i discovered the gateways which led me to encounter the human peopl
one of them killed another for some reason lmao
the controls on keyboard are so hard to get used to though im not gonna give up
very cool game

The best game I will never finish. If for some reason I ever get sick of abiding in an existence suffused with the nature of impermanence, and want to do the same thing but this time with a cute digital slugcat avatar, surrounded by some of the most fascinatingly lifelike digital creatures ever put to pixel, Rainworld is there for me.
game of the YEAR! cool slugcats cool regions cool everything. except for blue lizards they can go to hell

Yes.

After a full campaign with a couple other friends, I can wholeheartedly recommend this game to anyone. The platforming is incredibly fluid once you figure out how to control the slugcat. The graphics are also gorgeous; even though it's a pixelated game, it really doesn't feel that way when playing. The creature designs are also incredible and otherworldly.

It's not an easy game but you shouldn't be afraid of dying and having to restart a cycle. You rarely lose anything and what you do lose can be made back easily.

If you do get this game, I'd recommend getting the downpour DLC as well as the Rain Meadow mod which allows you to play online multiplayer.

10/10 game, there's nothing else like it.

You will find a lot of people discussing this games difficulty, rather than its qualities. The reason for this is simple. This game is atrocious. People keep talking about "brutality", or "no hand holding", like that's supposed to impress me. I do not care that it is hard, I do not care that you are good at it. Being "skill" based is not a feature worthy of praise, it is the baseline expectation for this genre of video game. The controls are simply awkward. Just because they are possible to master does not make it any less so. The player should want to learn your game and master it's controls through their own desire, because it's enjoyable, not because they are shamed into it. You are in no way obligated to enjoy this dreadful game. In addition, the procedural animation reduces the visual clarity of the game. The things you hold in your hand are obscured, and the enemy animations are muddy because every lifeform in this game is reduced to a blob being puppeted around. The effort put into the pixel art is grossly overshadowed by the characters constantly flopping and jittering with no personality or art direction. And lastly, there is hardly any variety in gameplay to be offered. Personal skill development is not a substitute for game design or progression. The miniscule amount of features this game has are stretched unimaginably thin through the mandatory retreading of areas. This will be one the most repetitive games you will ever play. If I was a conspiracy theorist, I would believe there is a guerilla marketing campaign designed to gaslight you into playing this game for more than 2 hours so you can't immediately refund it. No matter how technically brilliant the game might be, it is not fun. Its the equivalent to a Jacob Collier song, it's only good in theory.

Amazing cult classic, its hard asf but i'm in the cult and am unlikely to ever leave.

Downpour DLC is really good if you like the base game, The Watcher is really weird and I make no promises
Playing this game guides you to enlightenment, and let's just say I have ascended. I am no longer the same person I was before this game. Peak

this game is OKAY I GUESS,,, ignore the play time shhh

















































haha june fools this game is wonderful :D

There are good ideas in this game, but it gets buried in frustration and a gameplay loop which I think is not working as the developers intended.

You have to find food and hibernate to survive (not a spoiler, you learn this immediately). However, what I realized early on is that surviving is actually a bad strategy for completing the game. It's better to just die constantly which gives you much better time to explore the map, without worrying about neither food nor shelter. At some point you will need to survive to get past certain points, but it's better to just grind it out and find some food for those specific times, and ignore it and die left and right the rest of the time. Heck, I started using dying as a means to fast travel!

This makes for a very unrewarding and careless gameplay loop.

And the thing is, when you have to grind it out and maybe get some excitement as you try to evade predators and find the food you need, even then the game manages to get frustrating. You can randomly run into enemies that immediately kill you in the next room you enter, or even *invisible* enemies that you can't see. It is completely up to chance whether you make it or not. So it just feels unfair. 

Add to all of this that the controls are very janky as well, not giving you the control you need to survive when you find yourself in the many bad situations, and there is not much left to enjoy in this game. 

I will add that I loved the game for the first half hour, discovering its great atmosphere, before I discovered how bad the gameplay is.
You will die ALOT but it is still extremely enyojable and my advise is to not give up even if you die all the time
Its so immensely good the ecosystem is amazing and very realistic bought the dlc!!!

Amazing game. Would recommend for people who aren't as prone to raging. I've had to quit playing so I didn't crash out most of the time LOL. It's an amazing game and such an amazing learning curve too. Took me forever to get through the shaded citadel. Although, I use maps off the wiki because I'm not good at navigating most games. Either way, 10/10, I plan to get the DLCs eventually.
it is hard, but the good kind of hard. also complex environment, the good kind.

Iniciei o jogo.
iniciei nova campanha.
morri pra uma lagartixa mutante.
tentei de novo.
morri pra mesma lagartixa mutante.

10/10 recomendaria para um inimigo

before anything id like to say that this game doesnt play like a traditional game, im sure youve gathered from other reviews, but youre not going to be unlocking more power ups for your character as you progress and explore. this game may seem to be a metriodvania at first, but at most, it uses elements from the genre and nothing more.

either way, i havent enjoyed a game so much in ages.
its a difficult game, one thats much less forgiving than most, and some do say it can be unfair. which sure, but in my experience theres always a way to get around the issue you may get stuck at. sometimes you just need to take an alternate path. sometimes you need to make sure to save a spear, sometimes you just wait until the threat moves so you can sneak past. and sometimes the issue doesnt even appear despite being there the past 100 times you died to it. 
but anyway, the games beautiful, it makes good use of its soundtrack and long prevailed moments of no music that really helps enhance what would otherwise be a good soundtrack on its own. 

plus i like hard games that become easier when you just get more used to the environment it throws you in. because just like how you have so many weaknesses to the different animals, they all have some sort of behavoir to exploit. the noodle mosquitos for example, one of the few enemies that can dodge your spears intentionally. i found a good way to overcome this is by baiting them into a section of the map where youre forced to crawl, then throwing your spear at them then, as they dont have the space to dodge. 
its things like that you learn from playing and experimenting that really make this game fun for me.

there are also multiple campaigns to enjoy. the first is the longest and one of the best. but the hunter (second campaign) is a fun challenge with a sad but enjoyable story. 
for the dlc campaigns (except the watcher i havent finished that yet)
they act as shorter but still long (6 hours on average) to complete NG+ style experiences, with each having a unique ability and point in the timeline, with the best being the saint as you get a whole new fresh coat of paint for the whole map which is a lovely sight to take in at many points and makes the game feel fresh again if you played all the campaigns back to back immediately after beating the survivor campaign (the first one).

okay review to long now, fun game, hard game, beautiful environments and set pieces, interesting and not convoluted story, amazing learning curve based on patience and figuring things out yourself. 10/10 game for me personally

personally, just not my cup of tea, gameplay wise. the game sorta just drops you in, teaches you how to walk, and then [i] EVERYTHING[/i] after that is up to you to toy around with and figure out. the fanbase will vehemently suggest that you [i] not[/i] visit the game's wiki, or seek any sort of information outside of the game itself, in order to avoid spoiling the game's plot and rich lore for yourself.. but that's utterly impossible to do unless you have the patience of a [b] saint[/b][spoiler](haha, see what i did there??)[/spoiler], and have the time to sit with it long enough to dissect it.

i will, however, give it [i] MANY[/i] points for its incredible worldbuilding and [i] beautiful[/i] art direction. worth mentioning also is its emphasis on completely dynamic and realistic enemy ai and physics. if i remember right, the developer themself was dumbstruck when one of the enemies began, unpromptedly, using its tongue to traverse the landscape after all of its other appendages had become crippled. the game's ecosystem, and by extension, the creatures within it, all feel uniquely alive, and engage in shockingly realistic behavior. as for the physics, the game doesn't, to my knowledge, contain a single "hard-coded" animation. everything is simulated dynamically in real time, fluidly and elegantly.

i strongly recommend this game to those with a lot of patience and free time on their hands, who are willing to experiment and find out for themselves what works, and what doesn't. conversely, if you prefer games that guide your hand a little more towards the action, perhaps this game isn't for you.

It is very whimsical even if it repeatedly likes to kick your ass. I would recommend heavily if you like platformers and dont get too irritated at dying repeatedly. It is unforgiving and it is wonderful

I love you Rainworld. You are such a beautiful, artistic, piece. The surprise, wonder, nights I stayed under the covers lost in your wikis, reading about yet another creature page while being wildly depressed in first year of college-- life had gotten in the way but I keep coming back, again, and again. With watcher I have only felt the similar AWE, if not MORE, at the environments, the arts, the maze like layouts. WOW. I am so in love, and right now I am creating an animatic for this masterpiece. The lore is rich, the gameplay is brutal, humbling and hurts my ego. I fell in love in late 2023, and I cannot wait to think about this for another 10 years! 

I am really not a traditional gamer. Rarely a game feels SO alive, like piecing, responding, reacting to a piece of a ginormous painting. Thank you.

If this game was defined in 1 word it would be INTERESTING! From the creature designs, to the creativity of the world, to the story of a lost little slugcat trying to find his way home, and the in depth mechanics that make the world so alive this is WORTH the money! also really fun with friends
THIS GAME IS SO PEAK. I LOVE IT. It's very difficult and survival is very easy to learn. You can make friends with certain creatures, and the exploration is more than abundant.

I've played this on switch, and with how limited the switch is, playing on pc is LIFECHANGING. So, be prepared to die. A lot. This game is brutal, but it has an easy mode so it's not as brutal!!! !0/10 game would wawa again.

Probably my favourite game, the music, the environment, the lore, the enemies its great.
Also slugipede.

The game's really nice. I love the concept of custom/artificial ecosystems, so I ended up REALLY enjoying this game. Sure, being on the bottom of the food chain makes this game EXTREMELY hard and unforgiving, but that's just one side of the beauty this game offers. The artstyle is a breath of fresh air, and the procedural animation makes the creatures feel alive and the environment immersive. I'll admit, I beat the game with cheats, but even then it was still really hard. And the story, oh the story... I'm a lore enthusiast, which means I pay more attention to storytelling and the narrative, and oh man, Rain World has some of the deepest, most creative and detailed lore out of any game I've played. It is truly an amazing experience.

Would recommend. 8.5/10

(P.S. this game has an ungodly amount of mods in the workshop, which are further supported by the in game Remix mod so there's practically no way to run out of content)
I hated this game for the first 10 or so hours of it but once I started getting a hang of how the controls and enemies operate I couldn't put it down. It's hard but rewarding.

small disclaimer i'm glazing the shi out of rainworld

This game is absolutely unique and probably the most enjoyable game that i have ever played, it combines survival on a primal level with religion and existential dread, while managing to have beautiful graphics and having very atmospheric, immersive region design.

The music is also chef's kiss holy shi
And even though i thought it couldn't get better than this, the downpour dlc added so many new interpretations of the gameplay and the character of the slugcat itself, even though it might not be canon 
It does need much patience though because of the hard platforming and the oustanding creature AI, but otherwise I can only greatly recommend you to try it out yourself.

As of writing, I have gotten 2 endings in this game, and I am planning to finish every single campaign. This game I believe is a wonderful, wonderful piece of both game and art. However, despite me saying that I recommend it and giving it's praises, I do think it's far from a game for everyone. it is a tough and at times ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ game but at it's heart, the easiest and most consistent way to combat the enemies, doing the intense platforming, or navigating the map, is just having patience, and be willing to think outside the box at times. Not everyone has the patience to figure out how to deal with an enemy, or do a specific piece of platforming, which is completely fine, this game just isn't for you in that case. This game is immensely trial and error, it's best not to take deaths to heart and instead take them as a learning experience to understand why you died, and how it happened so you'll hopefully be able to learn and combat it. Also despite what people might say, combat is far from impossible, you may be prey but you are far from helpless against a predator if you know what your doing and you have the tools available (spear) to defend yourself or even in the cases of the more violent slug cats, turn the predator into prey.
i have 500 hours on my switch, rain world is the best game of all time, but if you dont like hard/frustrating games dont play it
This game simulates ecosystem really well. Creature behave very realistically. There are a lot of locations to explore. Surviving is really interesting

I love Rainworld. I started playing on the switch before I got it on steam because I didnt have a laptop at the time. While I dont care much about the story or campaigns, I love the open world. Most games dont give you as much exploration freedom and this game fills a void Ive been searching for since I was a young girl. I also love the difference options for the type of slugcats you can play as and how (assuming you have the downpour dlc) they arent locked behind their campaigns (once you beat them once of course, or unlock them in the menu). I also love how you can recolor them, which makes me feel as if they are more unique to me, making solo roleplaying even more fun. While the game is hard, over time you get use to the environment, making it more easy to traverse. I can wait to see what the devs create in the future and the dlcs we will probably get. I hope my review was helpful in some way since I know my lack of care for the story/campaign can be impactful, but i hope that my point of view was useful, even if my playing style is alot different then how your suppose to play it.
Absolutely amazing game, extremely hard. Do not play if you are frustrated easily.

this is probably the greatest game I've ever played. It inspired me to start making my own art, helped me form friendships in college, and really just overall had an incredible emotional impact on me. It's so fun, and spectacular, and awe-inspiring.

Quite possibly one of the best experiences i've ever had playing a game. 
Rain World literally puts you close to the bottom of the food chain, and you have to fight your way through a hostile and desolate world. 
A vast majority of the encounters in this game are emergent, the enemies move about in awkward ways that feel real. The story is absolutely unhinged and i love it. 10/10.

Rain World is a masterpiece of gaming. This is pure art in every aspect. The environments, the creatures, even your movement feels so weighted and connected to the world around you. The way you have to sneak, hide, decide when to risk a jump, you are the prey of this world, but you're smart too. The ending makes me emotional even though I most likely don't fully understand it yet even after all this time. This game is truly truly truly a work of art, and has so much heart and soul in it. Safe to say my favourite game ever.
Imagine the most painful, scary, dark, unnecessary, hurtful, boring, useless, wierd and ♥♥♥♥♥ place to exist and thats just memory crypts but way worse
10/10 would destroy an ecosystem again by speeding up a computer gods blue cancer while giving his big sis his brain cells. (until fluffy scug go boom)
Love the game, it is hard for new players and I stiil struggle since I'm not used to the controls. But, overall it is a fun game.
Slugcat can backflip so already a 10/10 and though it can be very challenging its really fun!

This game is evil. I hate you, evil evil you who made this accursed game. I hate this game so much I cannot even describe in words. This is the best game I’ve ever played, 10 out of 10. Made me want to die and I LOVE IIIITTTTT!!
tdrp here, i love this game too. you get to become a slug cat, and i love eating Ittm's brain. (i disagree with Ghost Kitty's review, Scavengers are the best and deserve lots of love and porls)

puts you on the edge of your seat more than any horror game could - don't die! in all seriousness though, amazing game so far (3 hours here and 5 switch) and one of its own in its class. there's nothing much like it out there

Rain world is a difficult game, which makes it hit-or-miss for some people. It takes patience and dedication but you will reap the rewards of getting more skillful and learning the complex lore. It's personally my favourite videogame and I have purchased and played it on multiple platforms already.
incredible game, took 7 hours to reach second zone without tutorial, died 4 times in a row within 5 minutes. 10/10 experience
Best game ever that you will probably hate! Play now, experience less pain later.
the game is good overall but i wish they would add more creatures to base game
perfect game to larp a family where the baby isnt quite the right color with your friends
I suck at parkour, but if you're good at that and like being consumed by flying and lizard like creatures, then go ahead and play this game.

I played this game for a few hours after I bought it and I didn't enjoy my time. After coming back to it a few months later I rediscovered a terrible game in my library and I can confidently say that Rain World has earned it's place as the worst game I've ever personally played.

The game play in Rain World is unlike anything that I've ever played. You can feel like the most powerful creature in the game while still knowing that you are only one mistake away from dying. The game is difficult in an unfair way that is very frustrating as it feels that you have no control over your environment. One of the worst parts of this game is it's simulated environments. Despite the fact that everything is simulated independently from you, it doesn't feel like you're just an observer in a real ecosystem. The creature AI in this game is really rudimentary and this ruins the feeling of an actual ecosystem with both predator and prey.

You play this game as a creature known as a slugcat. The worst feeling in this game is the feel of not getting stronger, not from in-game upgrades or weapons, but from your skill increasing and learning new mechanics and techniques with the tools available to you. Often, you'll think you've learned something, but you'll still die the same amount. This can make it feel like no matter how much progress you make, it just isn't beatable. The movement in this game is procedurally generated, which leads to the movement feeling unnatural or clunky, but then as you learn new movement techniques the movement becomes slightly more fluid and natural. Even with getting accustomed to it, the movement is some of the worst feeling movements in a game that I've ever played.

There's so much more to this game that I haven't covered here, but I'll leave that up to you to decide if you would like to try the game and become more familiar with it. This game is truly one-of-a-kind and itself is an experience. It certainly isn't for everyone.

Rain world isnt a game, its an EXPERIENCE that you have to get into to truly get all the satisfaction you need
Firstly, i played it, got to max karma (atleast the one which is possible in the outskirts) and didnt understand, what else?
But then i REALLY got into it, and, i mean, what can i say? this game is peak. In any way, shape, or form.
As a wise man once said "When life gives you lemons, dont make lemonade, make life take its lemons away"
Thats exactly what happens here! The game gives you all the tools that you need, so you make sure the game regrets doing it. A feeble spear can take more lives than youd think.
Overall, its peak, 9/10 when alive, 0/10 when dead (lol)

Simplesmente sensacional. O início é tortuoso, mas a sensação de curiosidade sobre o mundo e as criaturas é imensa. Ao longo do tempo, suas habilidades vão melhorando, assim como o seu conhecimento sobre tudo. Isso torna o jogo ainda mais legal, porque a partir daí você tem experiência e pode começar a criar estratégias para vencer criaturas específicas ou passar de certas áreas. A DLCs também valem muito a pena.
This game is amazing, i cannot stress enough how stunning this game is. It is incredibly hard, a real challenge, even for experienced gamers. Nonetheless an absolute blast. 9.5/10
This has been one of the most fun and also very challenging games I have played in a while and I am super glad to also be a part of the community!! :)
i mean hard game but with friends so much fun+mods are fun i got jukebox anywhere and i am now blasting hakari gambling theme in all bosses get rain medow for friends and jukebox anywhere for funzies
survivor campaign booring, if u hate solo games just get meadow mod (and a friend obviously), i hate daddy long legs

honestly? i don't think the art or the idea of the game is bad at all and it's not laggy on my potato pc, it's nice really, but having to fix all the controls was annoying for one, two, it just doesn't seem all that it's cut out to be. feeding before hibernation is also a tricky thing to do if the map is bigger, and i couldn't even climb up on some of the ropes because they were too high up it's just not my type of game.

THIS GAME IS THE MOST "FUN" game possible.   i first had this on XBOX/Nintendo  because my pc was broken and i beat all campains  even enots campain  and yes this game is hard well kinda depending on your skill/patence tbh.  this is a nice game fits the fact that i play hollow knight to often  i have like 10k hrs   on there   i only use the pc for moding i mean who cares  buy the game ok its good and i like explosions and lizards .  10 out of 10 now get a life
Ooooo You WILL watch way too many lore videos about this game after you play it and you totally won't become obsessed with the lore of this niche but peak asf game Ooooo

yeah pretty good game, its absolutely miserable but i love it, game forces you to learn on your own with little to no handholding, do not play this game if you dont like being lost and forced to learn on your own with no guides

the games good, but its quite painful to play, you WILL ragequit atleast once, theres no shame in downloading mods or playing as the monk, its a singleplayer game after all, do what makes you happy (unless you use jolly co-op or rain meadow)

overall, good game that makes you suffer. i like the slugpups :), and again, do not play this if you dont like being forced to learn without guidance, rain world is made to make you suffer, and make you learn from your previous deaths/mistakes. the cycle never ends. i like suffering. -a dyslexic person who cant properly explain things

It loves you. It hates you. It wishes nothing more than for your success. It will laugh as you fail for the tenth time in the same exact place. Rain World pushes both ways on purpose, to make you feel not as a protagonist, not as a survivor, but rather a frog in a rapid. It is through skill and knowledge that you persevere, that you leap to the next twig before the previous is torn to splinters. There is such a strange tier of progression to how your Slugcat "Learns" about "Things" that it is as unique as it is genius.
Rain World has the most complex ecosystem I have ever seen in a video game. It's incredibly difficult, but the story of each campaign is worth the time and money it takes to complete them.

Rain world is one of my favourite games of all time! (I even bought it on both console and pc) It's equally beautiful and brutal, and the creatures all interact with eachother like a real ecosystem where anything can happen. 

.. but king vultures and dropwigs can rot in a hole

Rain World is very strange for all the right reasons. Things will make sense, but often are not initially intuitive. The base game sees the truest form of what makes the core game loop so special. You are surviving, at odds with the world around you and its inhabitants. Through trial and error you learn to control your character, outsmart your enemies, and gain access to increasingly complex new areas, eventually begin unraveling a huge story narrative. The game is difficult and rewards persistence and curiosity. 

Both Downpour and Watcher DLCs bring with them new stories that bring exciting twists to the vanilla experience. With enough time all of this content now fully supports the additional game modes and multiplayer feature, this was one of the largest things the community seemed to hold against them. If you care about the lore in the sense of a cannon, I believe that both expansions will mostly fail to deliver on that, opting instead to explore side stories in different timelines (sort of kind of). Downpour combines the efforts of the community with some official oversight, while Watcher was done in house again. Out of the two, the Watcher stuck with me the most, but Downpour has more breadth of content by far.

Amazing graphics, insane lore, crazy freedom... This game has a TON of regions to explore, thousands of creatures and even multiple campaigns. The only downside is the pricing and the fact that the DLCs cost half the game price. I'd still buy them though.

Well this is long overdue

Rainworld came across my mind after a friend started talking about it. So after some time, and learning about the "push to meow" mod, I got the game.

Rainworld, aesthetically, is my favorite game of all time. The art is universally beautiful, even some of the weaker regions still look amazing. The game is so creative with everything it has. Plus when I got good, combat can be pretty alright too. The story is great, and while I didn't know everything about it at first, knowing what happened added a bigger sense of beauty to the game, not to mention the tragedy of [spoiler]Five Pebbles and Looks to the Moons.[/spoiler]

As for specific Slugcats, I'm just gonna make a list because I can't say everything in one paragraph.

Survivor - Basic white slug(cat), it was fun exploring the world for the first time as him. From learning the ropes in the Outskirts to being brutally castrated in [spoiler]Unfortunate Development[/spoiler] to [spoiler]descending into the Depths for the very first time and meeting the Void Worm[/spoiler], a good introduction to this world of rain.
(sorry monk and hunter, i had no interest in playing in either of you)

Gourmand - The fat ♥♥♥♥ was a good reintroduction into the game for me after I got the DLCs. I don't have much to say other than I'm confused on how he can just conjure children out of thin air by shoving two plants together. [spoiler]ALSO OH MY GOD SLUGPUPS ARE SO ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ CUTE[/spoiler]

Artificer - Probably my favorite scug gameplay wise. I already knew her story and the tragedy that followed suit, probably the fastest I've done any campaign at 24 Cycles my first time around. It truly made me good at combat.

Rivulet - the wet rodent. I have the least to say on Rivulet himself, but I can say is that the way his campaign develops both [spoiler]Five Pebbles and Moons[/spoiler] makes the tragedy in the base game even worse. I love both of them and I'm happy they got to reconcile.

Spearmaster - He's alright, the gimmick he has for eating is pretty cool. However, just like with Rivulet, it's the development on [spoiler]Pebbles and Moon[/spoiler] I think of his campaign about rather than the scug itself, despite him being very unique. [spoiler]The way Pebbles dug the hole deeper for himself when you get to him just makes me feel for him on a spiritual level, since it's something I do as well.[/spoiler]

Saint - The last one. Saint is a little hard to get used to due to his gimmick, however I think of his campaign so much due to the atmosphere of the world at this point in the timeline. [spoiler]Five Pebbles' megastructure collapsed, and now the world is covered in snow. When you get the ability to force-ascend creatures, it's a damn powertrip. It broke my heart seeing Pebbles in the state he was when I first found him in this campaign. When I got the ability to force-ascend, I set him and Moon free. AND SEEING THEM BOTH IN THE SAME ROOM IN RUBICON WAS SOME OF THE MOST HYPE STUFF EVER. And the part where Saint force-ascends the Void Worm is so.. Interesting, and it's cool that it's left up to interpretation on what Saint was doing by this.[/spoiler]

Watcher - The newest one. If I'm being honest, it took me some time to get around to getting some of the other endings in his campaign due to just being burnt out at this point. But today I got to it and man it was worth getting back into things. [spoiler]Watchers campaign was an emotional rollercoaster on the same level as Saint. From exploring a massive world, to seeing Spinning Top finally move on, to meeting the Prince, to being given the Weavers abilities and fixing the world from the Rot I unintentionally helped to spread. And then seeing Watcher dive into the Void Sea and become one of the Void Worms is the perfect example of how something can be terrifying and beautiful at the same time, which can just apply to the Void Sea in general if I'm being honest.[/spoiler]

This game is terrifying, yet there is beauty within that fear. If you have a high level of patience to withstand being instantly killed all of the time, check this game out.

You aren't a true rain world fan unless you hate the game.

This is an amazing game. It's incredibly punishing for the first hour or so while you're still learning the ropes, but after that point, the difficulty subsides as you get better and better at the game. Do not expect any powerups or anything of the sort, the only thing that improves throughout the game is your skill level and knowledge. 

Tips: 
0: IMHO the games controls are much easier to navigate when using a controller, keyboard just doesn't really mesh well with this.
1: Go in as blind as you can, but know that you are just a small part in a complex ecosystem.
2: Dying is okay, you don't really lose any progress when you die, and it's part of the learning process.
3: Pay attention to the environment, if you feel like you're stuck, just take a little bit to watch whatever you're stuck against, chances are, you'll notice a pattern you can exploit.

This game is absolutely ridiculous.
Rain World is an incredibly difficult game, and in a much different way than you might be used to if you've played hard games before. This world is unfair, and it does not care about you. I nearly gave up on this game in my first playthrough, something I've NEVER even considered before. You need lots of patience and lots of persistence to reach... well, anything.
But if you manage to become one of the few who see this game through, you'll find one of those fantastic moments that make the game worth playing, and you might just find what makes this game so special.

[h3] Rain world [/h3]
A game that I had been following for long time because of how it was developed. A game I've heard many talk about and praise. A game I've wanted to try for the longest time but never actually bought for whatever reason over the many others.

It took me a little bit to warm up to this world but the scugs had me tight in their slippery grip and wouldn't let go. Once I got somewhat used to the simultaneously punishing and forgiving cycles along with the albeit janky movement of every creature including yourself I couldn't let go either.

Several times, maybe completely unwarranted maybe not? this game actually managed to bring me to the verge of tears while playing.
I couldn't even tell you why. These weren't moments I died or even major moments of the story; these were moments I was just... living.
[i] and yes, this is a good thing. A very good thing [/i]

this game is amazing, id had it for less than a week and its already one of my favorite steam games, if your thinking of buying this game, note that: dying WILL happen, almost all enemies one shot, if you don't get mad at death, this could be one of your favorite games, i personally love being a killer, spearmaster if my favorite character to play as and i love the mods that give me balanced or straight overpowered slugcats to be, ill say it again, don't let death bother you, or you will hate this game, I've had the game for probably 3-4 days and I've died well over a hundred times, It's a fun open world game, it has a amazing story though i love the fact you can choose to ignore it and explore on your own, i find myself spending 3-4 hours on a single slot just roaming around, surviving the flood, and hunting other creatures, in all, this game is a fun game with a optional story mode, almost unlimited exploration, amazing challenges, but expect to die a LOT, the game holds your hand through absolutely none of it, and it does not holding back a single bit when it comes to the enemies
